results.name: bunnelab/mtbbench
results.description: MTBBench is a benchmark designed to evaluate the reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (LLMs) in complex clinical decision-making scenarios. It focuses on two core challenges in oncology: multimodal integration (e.g., pathology, genomics, radiology) and longitudinal reasoning across patient timelines.

results.name: dsi-clinical-llm/clinical-llm-evaluation
results.description: This repo is created to evaluate the LLMs on 4 tasks including Question-Answer (QA), Summarization, Name Entity Recognition (NER), and Relation Extraction (RE). The goal is to create a general framework to quickly evaluate any Causal Language Models against publicly available medical datasets.

results.name: bab-git/llm_pharma
results.description: This is a tutorial of an agentic Large Language Model (LLM) application to automate the evaluation of patients for clinical trials. It leverages documents related to patients' medical histories, clinical policies, and trial inclusion criteria.

results.name: hk21702/Evaluating-XAI-LLMs-in-a-Clinical-Context_CSC413-Project
results.description: A UofT CSC413 Final Project - Evaluating interpretable large language models in a clinical context using the MIMIC-IV dataset.

results.name: GOATnote-Inc/scribegoat2

results.name: Ufonia/wer-is-unaware
results.description: A benchmark, alignment pipeline, and LLM-as-a-Judge for evaluating the clinical impact of ASR errors.

results.name: Whitefield64/Comparing-Diagnostic-Reasoning-patterns-of-medical-specialist-and-LLMs
results.description: LLMs struggle with clinical adoption because their reasoning lacks transparency. Real medical diagnosis integrates abductive, deductive, and inductive logic. To bridge this gap, we must evaluate LLMs against an epistemic baseline grounded in human reasoning and structured for reproducibility.

results.name: UNHSAILLab/VSF-Med
results.description: We present VSF-Med, a Vulnerability Scoring Framework for medical Vision–Language Models (Vision LLMs), designed to systematically evaluate safety, reliability, and adversarial robustness in clinical imaging applications.
